steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: Before the blank is “Reclaim the,” and after is “an anthology of fantasy and science fiction written by authors of Latin American descent.” The latter phrase is not an independent clause—it describes what “Reclaim the [noun]” is.

steps.title: Apply the appositive rule
steps.content:
A noun phrase that describes or renames another noun is an appositive and requires a comma. The matching choice, “Stars, an,” correctly uses a comma to mark “an anthology of...” as an appositive explaining “Stars.”

The other choices fail by fragmenting the sentence, misplacing a conjunction, or omitting punctuation.

D is correct.
